new_classes.definition_en: The delimited geographical wine region of Tokaj (Tokaj-Hegyalja) in Hungary and Slovakia, whose wines are protected under EU wine law and referenced in Additional Note 4(B)(b) to Chapter 22 of the CN.

Wine of fresh grapes produced in the Tokaj wine region, meeting the conditions of Additional Note 4(B)(b) to Chapter 22 of the CN, presented in containers of 2 litres or less, and classified under CN subheading 2204 21 23.

coverage_explanation: The axioms capture the three core distinguishing criteria directly inferable from the CN code's position in the hierarchy: (1) the wine is from fresh grapes (via WineOfFreshGrapes parent), (2) it is produced in the Tokaj region (via hasProductionRegion some TokajRegion), and (3) it is presented in containers ≤ 2 L (via has_part some SmallContainer, inherited from the 2204 21 branch), and (4) it is a still (non-sparkling) wine (isCarbonated = false, distinguishing it from 2204 10). However, the detailed quality and compositional criteria specified in Additional Note 4(B)(b) to Chapter 22 — such as specific grape varieties, sugar content ranges, vinification rules, and PDO conditions for Tokaj — cannot be fully axiomatised from the referenced note text alone (the note text is not reproduced in full in the provided legal notes). Therefore coverage is partial; a full coverage would require the complete text of Additional Note 4(B)(b).
